step_by_step_thinking:
Hyaline membrane disease primarily affects premature infants due to incomplete alveolar surface active material production. This leads to decreased surfactant levels causing alveoli to collapse during exhalation, resulting in increased resistance to expiration and reduced compliance. As a result, the functional residual capacity (FRC) becomes less than the closing volume because the lungs cannot maintain adequate inflation after exhalation.
\boxed{A} Based on the provided evidence about hyaline membrane disease, we know that it involves inadequate surfactant leading to collapsed alveoli post-exhalation. Therefore, the functional residual capacity (FRC) would be smaller compared to the closing volume since the lungs struggle to stay fully expanded following exhalation.
